State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement . . . Conflict of Interest
(916) 322 5660                                    322 5901                    322 6441                372 5444

October 12, 1977


Senator John Garamendi
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear John:

     Thank you for your letter of September 28,
1977, regarding the filing deadlines for campaign
statements in local elections. I have asked a
member of our staff to contact you to explore the
problem and see whether any improvement in the law
is possible.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        Daniel H. Lowenstein
                                        Chairman

DHL:cjb

SENATOR'S ADDRESS
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO 95814

DISTRICT OFFICES
51 E. CHANNEL, ROOM 409
STOCKTON, CA 95202
(209) 948-7900

POST OFFICE BOX 80
MOKELUMNE HILL, CA 95245

SENATE COMMITTEES:
AGRICULTURE & WATER
RESOURCES
HEALTH & WELFARE
NATURAL RESOURCES
& WILDLIFE
PUBLIC UTILITIES, TRANSIT
& ENERGY

SENATOR JOHN GARAMENDI
THIRTEENTH DISTRICT

California Legislature

VICE CHAIRMAN
HEALTH AND WELFARE COMMITTEE

September 28, 1977

Mr. Dan Lowenstein, Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA    95804

Dear Mr. Lowenstein:

     It has come to my attention that in city and county elections
it is impossible to find out who is financing a campaign because
of the filing requirements.

     Under current law, the filing deadlines are spaced in such a
manner that they are ineffective for city races. This is because
in local races nearly all the money can be gathered or spent in
the last few days. Obviously, this is difficult if not impossible
in Assembly, Senate, and State-wide races.

     I would appreciate your thoughts on this matter and your
analysis of the situation with a view to changing the law so that
Prop 9 can be effective in local elections.

                                        Sincerely,


                                        JOHN GARAMENDI

JG/py

cc:  Peggy Mensinger
